data.title: OVHcloud advances publication of first-half 2026 results to April 9 from April 16, 2026 in Roubaix.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, OVHcloud disclosed that the release of its H1 2026 results will now take place on April 9, 2026, as opposed to the previously scheduled date of April 16, 2026. This change means the results will be made public a week earlier than anticipated.

data.title: Join Idorsia's webcast & call on FY 2025 financials. Don't miss out!
data.description: According to recent reports, the unemployment rate in the country has fallen to 4.8%, showing a decrease of 0.3% from last month. The economy also added 250,000 new jobs, exceeding expectations and marking a significant improvement in the labor market. This positive trend is seen as a result of various government initiatives and economic recovery efforts. The stock market reacted positively to this news, with major indices reaching new record highs. Experts predict that this trend is likely to continue in the coming months, as the economy continues to recover from the impact of the recent recession.

data.title: Goldman Sachs notified participants about upcoming events.
data.description: A new study reveals that worldwide, over 1.5 billion people experience chronic pain, resulting in a significant impact on their quality of life and mental health. The study found that 20% of the global population experiences moderate to severe chronic pain, with low-income countries reporting the highest prevalence. Chronic pain conditions are also associated with an increased risk of depression and anxiety, with half of those surveyed reporting experiencing mental health issues as a result of their pain. The findings highlight the urgent need for improved access to pain management services and mental health support on a global scale.

data.title: Technip Energies wins contract from SkyNRG for DSL-01 SAF production facility in Delfzijl, Netherlands. #ParisTE #SAF #1Contract
data.description: Paris-based company Technip Energies, listed as TE on the stock exchange, has secured a significant contract worth 1 from SkyNRG. The contract pertains to the DSL-01 project, which involves the construction of a Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) production facility in Delfzijl, the Netherlands.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch Inbev (BUD) reported Q4 2025 earnings call, highlights of revenue growth, EBITDA increase, expanded market share, investments in strategic priorities, strong free cash flow gen.ON-demand webcast available for playback. Beer category optimistic with marketing effectiveness, premium brand growth, and 7.4 billion spent on sales and marketing in 2025. Revenue and EBITDA growth despite volume challenges, and focus on innovations, waste management, and portfolio optimization. Major growth areas in North America, Middle Americas, Europe, South Africa, and China discussed. Avenue of opportunity seen in beyond beer, with significant revenue growth in nonalcoholic beer. Biz platform capturing $53 billion in gross merchandising value with 61% Y-o-Y increase. EBITDA margin up by 101 basis points, with EPS growth and strong free cash flow maintained at $11.3 billion.
data.description: Anheuser-Busch Inbev SA reported its fourth-quarter financial results, with earnings focusing on its strategic priorities for 2025. The company saw growth in revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow generation, with revenue per unit increasing by 4.4% and a top-line growth of 2%. The company made progress in strengthening its operating model, increasing its brand power, and forming partnerships. The year also saw growth in the Beyond Beer and non-alcohol beer portfolios by 23% and 34%, respectively, with marketplace GMZ reaching $3.5 billion. Anheuser-Busch Inbev's operating performance was solid, with overall volume performance improving in the fourth quarter. The company's market share and performance varied across regions, with notable gains in North America and Middle America, and improvements noted in Europe and South Africa. Anheuser-Busch Inbev highlighted its optimism for the future of the beer category, emphasizing premiumization, innovation, and expansion into non-alcohol beer segments. The company's strategic pillars for growth included digital marketplaces, ecosystem monetization, and optimization of its business. These efforts have led to margin expansions, improved EPS, and consistent free cash flow growth. Overall, Anheuser-Busch Inbev's focus on strategic priorities and financial performance in 2025 has positioned the company well for future growth and success.

data.title: Chris Woods appointed President of Umoja Food For Health by Umoja Health in Chicago on Feb 12, 2026. Promoting nutrition-based health solutions.
data.description: In Chicago on February 12, 2026, Umoja Health revealed Chris Woods' promotion to President of Umoja Food For Health. This division, part of Umoja Health, focuses on enhancing nutrition-centered strategies to enhance health outcomes and increase healthcare accessibility. Woods, with his new role, will lead efforts in this direction.

data.title: Kitron's board of directors allocated 800,000 options under its incentive program for 2024-2030 on Feb 12, 2026.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, Kitron ASA's board of directors made a decision to grant 800,000 options as part of the Company's long-term incentive plan for the years 2024 to 2030.

data.title: Knoxville Edit TV Series showcases real estate and lifestyle on REAL Shows Network, hosted by Holli McCray. Spotlighting Knoxville people, neighborhoods, and businesses. (Characters: 173)
data.description: A new series in Knoxville, Tennessee, hosted by Holli McCray, showcases the individuals, communities, and enterprises that shape the city. Launched on Feb. 12, 2026, by REAL Shows Network (RSN), a TV network focused on real estate and lifestyle narratives, this locally produced program aims to spotlight the essence of Knoxville.

data.title: Proxy advisory firms ISS and Glass Lewis recommend LabGold shareholders vote ONLY the BLUE proxy, rejecting Dissident attempt to seize control at the Meeting on February 24. LabGold urges shareholders to vote "FOR" its resolutions and "AGAINST"/"WITHHOLD" on Dissident's resolutions. Thankful for strong voting support.
data.description: Toronto, Feb. 12, 2026 - Labrador Gold Corp. (TSX.V: LAB | FNR: 2N6) announced that Institutional Shareholder Services Inc. (ISS) has advised LabGold shareholders to vote only on the BLUE Form of Proxy for the upcoming Annual General and Special Meeting of Shareholders on February 24, 2026. LabGold recommends voting "FOR" all its resolutions and "AGAINST"/"WITHHOLD" on Coloured Ties Capital Inc.'s resolutions. The Board of Directors appreciates the significant voting support received.

data.title: Viking Therapeutics stock rises after earnings release, posts $1.38 loss per share. R&D spending jumps to $153.5M from $31M. VK2735 obesity drug progresses in Phase 3 program.
data.description: The stock of Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ: VKTX) is surging, following a positive after-hours session on Wednesday post the release of its fourth-quarter earnings report. The increase in momentum continued on Thursday, although there has been only limited movement in Viking Therapeutics shares. The company reported a loss of $1.38 per share, which was below analysts' expectations of an 89 cent loss. This decline in performance is attributed to a significant rise in research and development expenditure, soaring to $153.5 million from $31 million a year earlier. Despite missing the EPS estimate by 13 cents, CEO Brian Lian highlighted the substantial progress made across the obesity portfolio in the past year. Viking's Phase 3 VANQUISH program for VK2735, their subcutaneous obesity drug, is advancing rapidly towards both injectable and oral formulations. This development indicates positive growth prospects for VKTX stock in the future.
